title: Bathing water quality monitoring for urban beaches
summary: A demonstrator using Sentinel-2 and Copernicus Marine Service data to flag potential water quality issues at urban bathing sites between official sampling dates.
narrative:
## What it does
This demonstrator screens Sentinel-2 optical imagery and CMEMS coastal water products for indicators associated with poor bathing water quality (turbidity, chlorophyll/algal bloom signals, river-plume intrusion after heavy rain) at urban beaches and bathing sites, complementing the periodic in-situ sampling required under the EU Bathing Water Directive.

## Why it matters
Official bathing water sampling happens on a fixed schedule, typically every 1-4 weeks in season. A satellite-based screening layer can flag anomalies between sampling dates, particularly after storm events that cause sewer overflow or runoff, giving municipalities an early signal to consider precautionary advisories.

## Status
This is a placeholder entry describing a demonstrator concept; no live instance is published yet.
